todaysEvents.summary: Israel's Defence Minister confirmed IRGC security chief Ali Larijani and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani killed in Israeli strikes on Mar 17; Trump told reporters Mojtaba Khamenei may be 'not alive' — Iran FM disputes, a statement was issued in Khamenei's name Mar 16; Iranian leadership structure under acute uncertainty. (Mar 17)
todaysEvents.delta: Larijani, Basij chief killed; Khamenei uncertain

todaysEvents.summary: Trump publicly scolded allies for refusing the Hormuz naval mission; EU foreign policy chief called it 'not Europe's war'; Germany and Australia formally declined; no country committed forces; Iran FM Araghchi: 'We never asked for a ceasefire.' Hormuz remains selectively closed under bilateral-deal-only framework. (Mar 17)
todaysEvents.delta: Coalition dead; allies refuse; no ceasefire

todaysEvents.summary: UAE sustained ongoing ballistic missile and drone strikes with one person killed in Abu Dhabi; Dubai Airport fire damage ongoing; Saudi Arabia intercepted 37 drones in eastern region; Lebanon death toll reached 850+ including 100+ children; Gulf-wide missile/drone campaign shows no sign of abating. (Mar 17)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran's selective-access bilateral transit framework persists despite leadership uncertainty following Larijani's killing. IRGC operations continue; Mojtaba Khamenei's disputed status has not triggered command breakdown. Brent oscillates $100-110/bbl; JKM stabilises near $19/MMBtu as Qatar restart continues and Australian LNG fills gaps. Trump coalition absent — US acting alone. P&I cover does not return broadly without a formal ceasefire framework.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Leadership disruption from Larijani's killing accelerates IRGC hardliner consolidation, collapsing bilateral transit deals. Brent retests $110-125/bbl; JKM grinds toward $22+/MMBtu with no Qatar full restart. Dubai Airport and Fujairah damage extend Gulf hub disruption beyond Hormuz. ASEAN oil-importer FX (THB, PHP, IDR) under sustained pressure; iTraxx Asia IG widens toward 175-190bp. Bank Indonesia, BSP, and BOT remain on intervention watch rather than easing.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Confirmed Khamenei death or IRGC leadership coup triggers uncontrolled escalation — full commercial Hormuz blockade reinstated. Additional GCC state infrastructure broadly targeted; US allies still absent. Oil hits $150-180/bbl; LNG spot markets seize for Asian buyers. ASEAN recession risk surges; Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and credit-rating action. P&I cover unavailable across the region for multiple quarters.
